result:
The final `annotations.json` is complete and validated. Here is a summary of the reconciliation:

**Final artifact: 26 row annotations, 4 pattern annotations, 2 unmapped observations**

**Reconciliation decisions:**

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Both reviewers agreed** | 13 rows (8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 36, 38) carried forward with merged checklist refs |
| **Rescued from pattern reviewer only** | Rows 7 and 10 (meals vendors coded Exempt when same vendor uses M&E elsewhere, verified against workbook) |
| **Both-sides additions** | Rows 26, 27, 31, 43-48 (Alphabet/Amazon HST ON counterparts) and rows 51, 52 (meals M&E counterparts) so every row in a conflicting-tax-code group shows the inconsistency |
| **Downgraded** | Row 38 downgraded to Needs More Context (both CHK-018 and CHK-020 concerns are speculative) |
| **Dropped** | CHK-005 removed from Amazon GST rows (GST is not exempt/zero-rated per CHK-005 criteria; CHK-007 covers the inconsistency) |

**Patterns:**
- **PAT-ALP-MIXED**: Alphabet three-way split (Exempt/GST/HST ON), 12 rows, strongest pattern
- **PAT-AMZ-MIXED**: Amazon GST vs HST ON across periods, 4 rows
- **PAT-MEALS-SPLIT**: T. Spaccio and Hungerhub Exempt vs M&E on same account, 4 rows
- **PAT-BILLEXP-SPLIT**: Billable Expense person-named (Exempt) vs company-named (HST ON), 4 rows
